channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Vance will meet Zelenskyy amid concerns about US-Russia talks to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Vice President JD Vance hammered home the U.S. demand that the NATO alliance step up defense spending on Friday, ahead of a security meeting in Europe at a time of intense concern and uncertainty over the Trump administration’s foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MATTHEW LEE and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone damaged the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thorny problems with Valentine’s roses: Invasive bugs and toxic pesticides hurt Kenya’s exports
item.description: This Valentine's Day Kenya's flower industry is being ravaged by pests, leading some flower farmers to rely more heavily on toxic pesticides.
item.dc:creator: Desmond Tiro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Armed fighters have raped scores of children in eastern Congo, UNICEF says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The U.N. children’s fund on Thursday accused armed men, likely on both sides of the conflict in eastern Congo, of raping scores of children over the past weeks as rebels expand their footprint and push government forces out.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong plans to ban substandard tiny apartments. Low-income families fear higher rents
item.description: Hong Kong plans to crack down on poor-quality subdivided flats are leaving many low-income residents uncertain about their future in one of the world’s most expensive housing markets.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US deports 119 migrants from a variety of nations to Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama has received the first U.S. flight carrying deportees from other nations as the Trump administration takes Panama up on its offer to act as a stopover for expelled migrants, the Central American nation’s president said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 4 dead, 26 hurt in Taiwan food court explosion
item.description: Four people were killed and another 26 were injured following a Thursday explosion at the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi department store in Taichung, Taiwan, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants denuclearization talks with Russia and China, hopes for defense spending cuts
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he wants to restart nuclear arms control talks with Russia and China and that eventually he hopes all three countries could agree to cut their massive defense budgets in half.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Russian-American ballerina 'in transit' after US says she was wrongfully detained, boyfriend says
item.description: Russian-American ballerina Ksenia Karelina, who was imprisoned in Russia after donating just over $50 to a Ukrainian charity last year, is “in transit," her boyfriend tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's Macron urges Syria's interim government to join a US-led coalition fighting extremists
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron urged Syria's interim government to cooperate with a U.S.-led coalition fighting against extremist groups in that region as he hosted a conference Thursday on the Mideast country's future.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, ABBY SEWELL and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Hamas hostage's mom says daughter was forced to fake her death for a propaganda video
item.description: Orly Gilboa, mother of former Hamas hostage Daniella Gilboa, said her daughter was forced to fake her own death and was covered in debris for a propaganda video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth: 'No betrayal' in US position on Russia, Ukraine
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said there’s “no betrayal” in the U.S. position on ending the war in Ukraine, as the Trump administration pushes for negotiations between Russia and the neighboring country it invaded nearly three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: How South Korea's Constitutional Court is deciding on the president's future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is putting up a desperate fight for his political life at Seoul’s Constitutional Court after being impeached and arrested for his short-lived imposition of martial law last year.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elephants trumpet, squeak and flap their ears after their complex move across an Australian city
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Elephants trumpeted, touched trunks and flapped their oversized ears upon reuniting with their herd after a complex, five-day move from an urban Australian zoo to a much larger enclosure.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: A joke petition seeks to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation
